basics.name: Muhammad Fareed
basics.label: Machine Learning & Artificial Intelligence Researcher
basics.summary: Machine learning and AI researcher working on efficient, deployable AI — parameter-efficient fine-tuning (QLoRA/LoRA), quantization, and small/multimodal models that run on modest hardware. Healthcare and low-resource-language AI are the primary proving grounds; the methods generalize across domains.

education.institution: Riphah School of Computing & Innovation (RSCI), Riphah International University, Lahore
education.score: CGPA 4.0/4.0 — batch topper (first position, Spring 2024 cohort); full scholarship throughout. Dean's Honor List (Certificate of Merit Achievement), Spring 2024. Thesis: Retrieval-Augmented, Parameter-Efficient Fine-Tuning of a Vision–Language Model for Chest X-ray Report Generation (supervisor: Dr. Jamal Uddin).
courses: Statistical & Mathematical Methods for Data Science (A)

education.area: B.Ed. Secondary (1.5-Year) — Specialisation: ICT in Education

education.institution: University of Education, Township Campus, Lahore
Punjab College of Science, Muslim Town Campus, Lahore
Aziz Memorial Science High School, Lahore
education.score: Grade A+ (954/1050, ≥90.9%) — first position at school level

publications.name: A Systematic Review of Ethical Considerations of Large Language Models in Healthcare and Medicine
publications.publisher: Frontiers in Digital Health (IF 3.8, Q1) — Published, first author
publications.summary: Fareed, M., Fatima, M., Uddin, J., Ahmed, A., & Sattar, M. A. A PRISMA 2020 systematic review of ethical risks in deploying LLMs in healthcare; 27 studies included; proposes a four-layer ethical integration framework.

publications.name: CXRG-SVLM: A Parameter-Efficient Small Vision-Language Model for Multi-View Chest X-ray Report Generation in Resource-Constrained Environments
publications.publisher: Scientific Reports — under review (MS thesis work), first author
publications.summary: Fareed, M., Uddin, J., Ahmed, A., Sayyid, A. H., & Sattar, M. A. Small VLM generating chest X-ray reports on a single 16 GB GPU; 4-bit QLoRA (0.97% trainable); ROUGE-L 0.3116, METEOR 0.2530, clinical-entity F1 0.4867.

publications.name: Optimizing Multilingual BERT for Punjabi Sentiment Analysis using PEFT Techniques
publications.publisher: Computers, Materials & Continua (CMC), Tech Science Press — under review, co-author
publications.summary: Ahmed, A., Riaz, S., & Fareed, M. (third author). First systematic PEFT benchmark for Punjabi sentiment analysis; LoRA 85% accuracy at 1.07% trainable parameters.

publications.name: A Systematic Review of LSTM-Based Deep Learning Models in Healthcare and Medicine
publications.publisher: In preparation (target: Frontiers) — not yet submitted, first author
publications.summary: Fareed, M., Uddin, J., Ahmed, A., Nauman, M. A., Khan, N., Billah, M., & Sattar, M. A. PRISMA 2020 systematic review of LSTM applications across healthcare; 101 studies; identifies six advancement clusters.

work.name: Govt. Elementary School, Attari Saroba, Lahore
work.summary: Substantive permanent civil-service post (awarded by competitive examination), held concurrently with MS studies, thesis research, and four peer-reviewed papers (2024–2026).

awards.title: Inaugural cohort — AI-Driven Enterprise Product Development Programme
awards.awarder: Prime Minister's Youth Programme (PMYP) × Stanford Engineering CGOE
awards.summary: Competitive national selection; cohort developed and deployed AI-driven enterprise solutions under Stanford faculty guidance.

awards.title: First position (batch topper), MS Data Science — Spring 2024 cohort
Dean's Honor List (Certificate of Merit Achievement), MS Data Science
First position at school level — SSC (Science, Grade A+)
awards.awarder: Aziz Memorial Science High School, Lahore

certificates.name: Generative AI Engineering and Fine-tuning Transformers

volunteer.summary: Welfare and development initiatives for children with physical and cognitive disabilities; co-managed organisational programmes and community outreach.

references.name: Dr. Jamal Uddin
references.reference: Assistant Professor; Head of Dept., AI & Data Science, RSCI, Riphah International University. MS thesis supervisor; taught Deep Learning and Statistical & Mathematical Methods. jamal.din@riphah.edu.pk

references.reference: Associate Professor; Head of Dept., Software Engineering, RSCI, Riphah International University. Taught Advanced Database Systems and Cloud Computing Infrastructure & Services. muhammad.yaseen@riphah.edu.pk

references.name: Dr. Muhammad Awais Sattar
references.reference: Postdoctoral Researcher, Luleå University of Technology, Sweden. Taught Tools & Techniques in Data Science, Programming Fundamentals, and Research Methods; corresponding author on two papers. muhammad.awais.sattar@associated.ltu.se
